ticker.text: Raptors clinch 5th seed — Round 1 vs. Cavaliers starts Saturday
George Springer (fractured toe) placed on 10-day IL
Commanders pick #7 overall — April 23 in Pittsburgh
Blue Jays at Brewers — 7:40 PM ET on SN

featured.headline: The Raptors Are Back. First Playoff Appearance Since 2022 — Cavaliers Await.
featured.dek: Toronto clinched the 5th seed and will face the 4th-seeded Cleveland Cavaliers in Round 1. Behind Scottie Barnes and Brandon Ingram, the Raptors closed the regular season strong. Game 1 tips off Saturday at 1 PM in Cleveland — two deep rosters and a series that could go the distance.

two_up.headline: Defending AL Champs Drop to 6–9 After Twins Take Series — Milwaukee Tonight
two_up.dek: Minnesota hammered Toronto 8-2 on Sunday to take the series. With Springer on the IL and the lineup scuffling, the Jays head to Milwaukee tonight needing Gausman to right the ship.

two_up.headline: Bourque's Hat Trick Sinks Leafs in Final Home Game — Six Straight Losses
two_up.dek: Dallas rallied from a 5-3 deficit with three goals in 6:34 — Mavrik Bourque completing his first NHL hat trick. Jacob Quillan scored his first NHL goal in a 6-5 loss that sent the home crowd off on a sour note.

extra_story.headline: Commanders Reportedly Seeking Trade Down From #7 — Nine Days Until Pittsburgh
extra_story.dek: GM Adam Peters wants more picks after trading away the 2nd and 4th rounders for Tunsil. A WR opposite McLaurin remains the target — Carnell Tate, Makai Lemon, and Omar Cooper Jr. are all in play.

today_slate.detail: Off day — at Ottawa tomorrow 7:30 PM
Playoffs begin Sat Apr 18 — Game 1 at Cleveland

week_ahead.note: NFL Draft: Apr 23–25 in Pittsburgh · Round 1 Thu 8 PM ET on ESPN / NFL Network

leafs.detail: Eliminated from playoffs · First miss since 2015–16

lotl.label: What's Going On With the Leafs
lotl.body: Six straight losses and counting — the Leafs are limping to the finish line. Sunday's 6–5 loss to Dallas was a wild one — Gabriel Bourque scored a hat trick for the Stars, while William Nylander answered with a goal and two assists and rookie Nick Quillan potted his first NHL goal. But it wasn't enough, and the Leafs' season-ending skid is now 0–5–1 over the last six. Toronto is 32–36–14, locked into a bottom-five finish and already looking ahead to a franchise-altering offseason. The Matthews MCL tear, the Marner trade, the Treliving firing — it all adds up to the most consequential summer in a decade. Two games remain: at Ottawa Wednesday, then home to Montreal Saturday to close it out. The only suspense left is draft lottery positioning and whether Nylander — 75 points and climbing — stays or goes.

key_numbers.label: Nylander Pts
key_numbers.note: 27 G, 48 A — team leader by a mile

key_numbers.note: at Ottawa Wed, vs. Montréal Sat

the_latest.headline: Bourque's hat trick sinks Leafs 6–5 as losing streak hits six — Nylander and Quillan score in wild loss
the_latest.dek: Gabriel Bourque scored three for Dallas in a wild back-and-forth affair. William Nylander had a goal and two assists, and rookie Nick Quillan scored his first NHL goal, but the Leafs' slide continues.

the_latest.headline: Why the Leafs missed the playoffs: Marner's departure, Matthews' MCL tear, defensive woes
the_latest.dek: Mitch Marner's sign-and-trade to Vegas, Auston Matthews' season-ending MCL injury on March 13, and the firing of GM Treliving on March 30 tell the story of a season that fell apart.

the_latest.headline: Eliminated from playoff contention for the first time since 2015–16
the_latest.dek: A loss to San Jose combined with wins by Detroit and Ottawa sealed it on April 2 — ending the longest active playoff streak in the NHL at nine years.

jays.detail: 4th in AL East · 50th Anniversary Season · Defending AL Champions

lotl.label: What's Going On With the Blue Jays
lotl.body: The early-season slide keeps getting uglier for the defending AL champs. The Twins took the series with an 8–2 blowout on Saturday, dropping Toronto to 6–9 — four games back of a wild card spot and already in catch-up mode barely two weeks in. The IL is overflowing: Springer (fractured toe), Kirk (thumb surgery), Barger (ankle), and Ponce (ACL) have roughly $100M in combined salary sitting on the shelf. Vladimir Guerrero Jr. has been a bright spot at the plate, but the pitching staff beyond the top two has been shaky, and the bullpen blew Saturday's game open with four runs in the eighth. Tonight it's at Milwaukee (7:40 PM, Sportsnet) — Gausman vs. Misiorowski to open a three-game set. The Jays need a strong road trip through Milwaukee and Arizona to keep this from spiraling into a real hole before May. At some point the talent will catch up to the record, but that point keeps getting pushed back.

key_numbers.note: 4 games back in the AL wild card race

key_numbers.label: Game Skid
key_numbers.note: Lost 3 of last 4 heading to Milwaukee

the_latest.headline: Twins rout Blue Jays 8–2 to take series — Toronto drops to 6–9 heading to Milwaukee
the_latest.dek: Minnesota blew the game open with four runs in the eighth. The Jays' bullpen crumbled and the bats went cold, sending the defending AL champs to their third loss in four games.

the_latest.headline: George Springer fractures toe, placed on 10-day IL — Eloy Jiménez called up
the_latest.dek: Springer fouled a ball off his left foot in Saturday's 7-4 loss to the Twins. He's hitting .189/.283/.377 and now joins Kirk, Barger, and Ponce on an injury list approaching $100M in salary.

the_latest.headline: Valenzuela's first MLB homer powers Jays to 10-4 rout of Twins
the_latest.dek: Brandon Valenzuela launched his first career home run and Daulton Varsho added another as Toronto halted Minnesota's four-game win streak on Friday night.

raptors.detail: 5th Seed East · First Playoffs Since 2022 · vs. #4 Cleveland Cavaliers

live_strip.text: Round 1 vs. Cleveland Cavaliers starts Saturday — first postseason since 2022

lotl.label: What's Going On With the Raptors
lotl.body: This is the good-news story of the spring. The Raptors are in the playoffs for the first time since 2022, and they earned it the hard way — closing the regular season at 46-36 to lock up the 5th seed in the East on the final day with a win over Brooklyn. Scottie Barnes has been sensational down the stretch, dropping 25 points in a dominant 121-95 win over Miami that effectively sealed the spot. Brandon Ingram, in his first year in Toronto, has been the perfect second star — including a 38-point eruption against the Heat earlier in the week. The reward? A Round 1 date with the 4th-seeded Cleveland Cavaliers, who finished 51-31 behind Donovan Mitchell and Jarrett Allen. Cleveland is a tough draw, but this Raptors team is peaking at the right moment — they shot 51.5% from the field over their last 10 games and have the defensive tools to make things uncomfortable. Game 1 tips off Saturday, April 18 at 1 PM ET at Rocket Mortgage FieldHouse on ABC / TSN. Four days to get ready. It's going to be a war.

key_numbers.label: Ingram PPG
key_numbers.note: First year in Toronto — perfect fit

the_latest.headline: Raptors clinch 5th seed, draw Cavaliers in Round 1 — first playoffs since 2022
the_latest.dek: Toronto closed the regular season at 46-36, securing the 5th seed with a final-day win over Brooklyn. The 4th-seeded Cleveland Cavaliers await — Game 1 is Saturday at 1 PM ET.

the_latest.headline: Scottie Barnes drops 25 as Raptors rout Heat 121-95, move one step closer
the_latest.dek: Barnes led a dominant performance as Toronto moved within striking distance of clinching. Brandon Ingram had 38 in an earlier 128-114 Heat win. This team is peaking at the right time.

the_latest.headline: The Cavaliers matchup plays into Toronto's strengths — can they pull the upset?
the_latest.dek: With elite defensive tools and Scottie Barnes playing at an All-Star level, the Raptors have the pieces to make this a long series against Donovan Mitchell and Cleveland.

live_strip.text: Pick #7 overall in Pittsburgh — reportedly seeking trade down · WR opposite McLaurin the target

lotl.label: What's Going On With the Commanders
lotl.body: Nine days until the draft, and the buzz is building. Reports surfaced Monday that Washington is actively seeking a trade down from the #7 pick — GM Adam Peters wants to recoup the 2nd-round pick he gave up in the Tunsil trade while still landing a top receiver prospect. Carnell Tate (Ohio State), Makai Lemon (USC), and Omar Cooper Jr. (Indiana) have all made pre-draft visits, and a slide into the 12–18 range could still net one of them. Meanwhile, the offseason overhaul continues to take shape: 15 new signings headlined by a $100M pass rusher, new DC Daronte Jones installing his scheme, and Daniels and McLaurin healthy and working out together in LA. OTAs kick off next Monday (April 20), and the draft is April 23–25 in Pittsburgh. Six total picks, no 2nd or 4th rounder. If Peters can engineer a trade-down that adds picks without sacrificing his WR target, this could be the move of draft weekend.

the_latest.headline: 2026 NFL Draft: Commanders updates, intel, buzz — picks & positions
the_latest.dek: Six total picks, headlined by #7 overall. WR is the target — Carnell Tate (OSU) and Makai Lemon (USC) among top visits. Peters open to trading down.

the_latest.headline: Washington's 2026 NFL Draft picks have been finalized
the_latest.dek: Picks at 7, 71, 147, 187, 209, 223. No 2nd or 4th rounder after the Tunsil trade. Extra 6th from the Robinson deal with SF.

the_latest.headline: OTAs start April 20: New DC Daronte Jones, $100M pass rusher, and a roster overhaul
the_latest.dek: Former Vikings DB coach Jones takes over as DC. A major pass-rusher signing ($100M, $68M guaranteed) headlines 15 new faces as Peters got younger and faster.

the_latest.headline: Jayden Daniels and Terry McLaurin healthy heading into OTAs
the_latest.dek: Injuries to both derailed 2025. Full health is the biggest offseason storyline — and the biggest reason for hope. Plus: Tunsil signs a multi-year extension.
